decision_question: Should we invest in or prepare for nuclear fusion as a commercially viable energy source within the next 15 years?

hypotheses.hypothesis: Nuclear fusion will achieve grid-connected electricity at a levelized cost below $50/MWh by 2050.
hypotheses.decision_relevance: Determines if fusion can compete with renewables and natural gas.
needed_evidence: LCOE projections from fusion developers and independent studies; cost trends for key components (magnets, lasers, tritium).
search_queries: levelized cost of fusion energy report

hypotheses.hypothesis: At least one private fusion company will demonstrate net electricity generation to the grid by 2035.
hypotheses.decision_relevance: Validates near-term commercial feasibility and investment timing.
needed_evidence: Development timelines from leading companies (Commonwealth Fusion, TAE, Helion); regulatory approvals for grid connection.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Government funding for fusion R&D will exceed $10 billion globally by 2030.
hypotheses.decision_relevance: Indicates policy commitment and potential for accelerated development.
needed_evidence: National fusion budgets (US, EU, Japan, China, UK); private investment data.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Fusion will not achieve commercial viability before 2045 due to unresolved materials and tritium breeding challenges.
hypotheses.decision_relevance: Challenges optimistic timelines and affects investment horizon.
needed_evidence: Peer-reviewed studies on tritium breeding ratio, neutron damage to materials; expert surveys.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Private investment in fusion will exceed $5 billion cumulative by 2025.
hypotheses.decision_relevance: Measures market confidence and capital availability.
needed_evidence: Venture capital and corporate investment data; Fusion Industry Association reports.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Fusion power plants will have a capacity factor above 90%, making them competitive with nuclear fission.
hypotheses.decision_relevance: Impacts LCOE and grid reliability value.
needed_evidence: Design capacity factor projections from developers; comparison with fission and renewables.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Regulatory frameworks for fusion will be harmonized globally by 2040, reducing deployment delays.
hypotheses.decision_relevance: Affects speed of commercialization and market access.
needed_evidence: Status of fusion regulatory frameworks in US, UK, EU; IAEA activities.

market_sizing_plan.sizing_question: How large is the credible opportunity for Nuclear fusion commercialization outlook and strategic implications, and which variables still need validation?

methods.formula: Total addressable market (TWh) = Global electricity demand (TWh) × fusion penetration rate (%)
known_limitations: Penetration rate is highly uncertain; depends on cost competitiveness and policy.

methods.formula: Market size (GW) = Number of fusion plants × average plant capacity (GW)
variables: Number of fusion plants (based on developer announcements and build rates)
Average plant capacity (e.g., 500 MW to 1 GW)
known_limitations: Few concrete plant announcements; many projects are pre-commercial.

methods.formula: Adopted capacity (GW) = (Number of pilot plants × success rate) × (scale-up factor) × (commercial adoption rate)
variables: Commercial adoption rate (based on LCOE and grid needs)
known_limitations: Success rates are speculative; scale-up factors unknown.

methods.formula: Annual revenue ($) = Market size (TWh) × wholesale electricity price ($/MWh)
variables: Market size from top-down or bottom-up
known_limitations: Electricity prices are volatile and region-dependent.

market_sizing_plan.evidence_rule: Use public source values when available; keep missing variables as validation tasks.

validation_data_needs.decision_use: Quantify TAM/SAM before writing a growth claim.
search_queries: Nuclear fusion commercialization outlook and strategic implications market size forecast data

validation_data_needs.decision_use: Cross-check revenue pools with physical or usage demand.
search_queries: Nuclear fusion commercialization outlook and strategic implications demand volume official data
Nuclear fusion commercialization outlook and strategic implications customer count users

validation_data_needs.decision_use: Estimate realistic conversion from demand to revenue.
search_queries: Nuclear fusion commercialization outlook and strategic implications adoption rate penetration
Nuclear fusion commercialization outlook and strategic implications price ARPU ASP

validation_data_needs.decision_use: Test whether the opportunity can earn attractive economics.
search_queries: Nuclear fusion commercialization outlook and strategic implications cost CAPEX OPEX

validation_data_needs.decision_use: Check whether supply can serve demand.
search_queries: Nuclear fusion commercialization outlook and strategic implications capacity project pipeline

validation_data_needs.decision_use: Validate capital formation and investor conviction.
search_queries: Nuclear fusion commercialization outlook and strategic implications funding investment by company
Nuclear fusion commercialization outlook and strategic implications regulation policy approval

validation_data_needs.decision_use: Separate market growth from player-level advantage.
search_queries: Nuclear fusion commercialization outlook and strategic implications market share competitive landscape

validation_data_needs.decision_use: Verify that adoption exists outside narrative claims.
search_queries: Nuclear fusion commercialization outlook and strategic implications customer case order announcement

validation_data_needs.decision_use: Anchor the commercialization clock in dated evidence.
search_queries: Nuclear fusion commercialization outlook and strategic implications milestone timeline commercialization

chart_data_needs.title: Fusion LCOE Projections vs. Solar, Wind, and Natural Gas (2025-2050)
chart_data_needs.executive_question: Can fusion ever be cost-competitive with renewables?
required_metrics: Fusion LCOE ($/MWh) projections for 2025, 2030, 2035, 2040, 2045, 2050
Solar PV LCOE ($/MWh) projections for same years
Onshore wind LCOE ($/MWh) projections for same years
Natural gas combined cycle LCOE ($/MWh) projections for same years
comparison_set: Fusion vs. solar, wind, natural gas
preferred_sources: Lazard Levelized Cost of Energy Analysis
Fusion developer reports (Commonwealth Fusion, TAE, Helion)
search_queries: IEA World Energy Outlook 2024 LCOE projections
chart_data_needs.data_quality_rule: Use only publicly available LCOE estimates from authoritative sources; if fusion LCOE data is unavailable, note as 'not yet publicly disclosed'.

chart_data_needs.title: Timeline of Key Fusion Milestones vs. Actual Progress
chart_data_needs.executive_question: How realistic are current commercialization timelines?
required_metrics: Date of first net energy gain (e.g., NIF 2022)
Date of sustained burn (e.g., >5 minutes)
Date of first grid-connected electricity (planned)
Date of commercial plant operation (planned)
Actual milestone achievement dates vs. planned dates
comparison_set: Planned vs. actual milestones for leading projects (SPARC, STEP, DEMO, Helion)
preferred_sources: Company press releases and roadmaps (Commonwealth Fusion, UKAEA, Helion)
DOE Office of Science fusion announcements
chart_data_needs.data_quality_rule: Use official company or government announcements; if a milestone is only projected, label as 'planned'.

chart_data_needs.title: Global Fusion Funding (Public + Private) by Year and Country
chart_data_needs.executive_question: Is the funding trend sufficient to accelerate development?
required_metrics: Annual public fusion R&D funding ($M) for US, EU, Japan, China, UK, 2015-2025
Annual private fusion investment ($M) globally, 2015-2025
Cumulative public and private funding by country
comparison_set: Public vs. private funding by country
preferred_sources: Japan Ministry of Education fusion budget
search_queries: EU fusion program funding Horizon Europe
chart_data_needs.data_quality_rule: Use official budget documents and industry reports; if data for a specific country is unavailable, exclude that country.

chart_data_needs.title: Adoption Funnel: From Pilot Plants to Commercial Deployment Scenarios
chart_data_needs.executive_question: What is the likely market penetration by 2050?
required_metrics: Number of pilot fusion plants planned (e.g., SPARC, STEP, DEMO, Helion Polaris)
Expected capacity per pilot plant (MW)
Estimated success rate of pilot to commercial (e.g., 50%, 70%)
Commercial plant capacity (MW) and number by 2050 under different scenarios
preferred_sources: Expert elicitation studies (e.g., from Nature or Energy Policy)
search_queries: fusion pilot plant list capacity MW
fusion DEMO to commercial scale-up factor
chart_data_needs.data_quality_rule: Use only announced pilot plants; success rates must be sourced from peer-reviewed studies or expert surveys.

chart_data_needs.title: Regulatory Readiness Map: Countries with Fusion Licensing Frameworks
chart_data_needs.executive_question: Which markets are prepared for fusion deployment?
required_metrics: Country (US, UK, EU, Japan, China, Canada, Australia)
Fusion regulatory framework status (none, in development, established)
Year framework established or expected
Key regulatory body (e.g., NRC, ONR, ASN)
preferred_sources: US Nuclear Regulatory Commission fusion rulemaking
UK Department for Business, Energy & Industrial Strategy fusion regulation
search_queries: IAEA fusion regulation status by country
chart_data_needs.data_quality_rule: Use official government or regulatory body publications; if status is unclear, label as 'in development'.

chart_data_needs.title: Private Investment in Fusion by Company (Cumulative to 2025)
chart_data_needs.executive_question: Which companies are attracting the most capital?
required_metrics: Company name (Commonwealth Fusion, TAE, Helion, General Fusion, Zap Energy, etc.)
Cumulative private investment ($M) as of 2025
Year of largest funding round
Investor type (VC, corporate, government)
chart_data_needs.data_quality_rule: Use verified funding data from multiple sources; if a company's total is undisclosed, note as 'not publicly disclosed'.

chart_data_needs.title: Fusion Capacity Factor Projections vs. Nuclear Fission and Renewables
chart_data_needs.executive_question: Will fusion have a capacity factor advantage?
required_metrics: Fusion capacity factor (%) projection (e.g., 90-95%)
Nuclear fission capacity factor (%) actual (e.g., 92% in US)
Solar PV capacity factor (%) actual (e.g., 15-25%)
Onshore wind capacity factor (%) actual (e.g., 30-40%)
chart_data_needs.data_quality_rule: Use actual historical data for fission and renewables; fusion projections must be sourced from developer or independent studies.

storyline_plan.core_question: Should we invest in or prepare for nuclear fusion as a commercially viable energy source within the next 15 years?
storyline_plan.central_thesis: The storyline should be led by the strongest public evidence, then translated into management choices.
storyline_plan.narrative_focus: Evidence is densest around: funding, timeline, adoption, technology
storyline_plan.structure_logic: Open with the executive decision, show source-backed data exhibits, then move through business implications, risks and action gates.
evidence_must_cover: To date, Milestone awardees have collectively raised over $350 million of new private funding since their selection into the program was announced in May 2023, compared to the $46 million of federal funding initially committed for negotiated milestones.

To date, Milestone awardees have collectively raised over $350 million of new private funding since their selection into the program was announced in May 2023, compared to the $46 million of federal funding initially committed for negotiated milestones.

Highlights of the FY 2027 Request The FES FY 2027 Request of $755.3 million is a decrease of $50.406 million below FY 2026 Enacted, with reduced funding for core research to offset increases for high priority research activities and facility operations.

Highlights of the FY 2027 Request The FES FY 2027 Request of $755.3 million is a decrease of $50.406 million below FY 2026 Enacted, with reduced funding for core research to offset increases for high priority research activities and facility operations.

All 8 awardees are presently working toward presenting pre-conceptual designs and technology roadmaps of their FPP concepts within the first 18 months of the Milestone program—roughly late 2025 (18 months into the Milestone Program).

Department of Energy (DOE) today announced $107 million in funding for six projects in the Fusion Innovative Research Engine (FIRE) Collaboratives , and that several privately funded fusion companies have completed early critical-path science and technology (S&T) milestones in the Milestone-Based Fusion Development Program (“the Milestone Program”).
